text:
Once upon a time in a little village nestled among rolling hills were five sisters. They were known far and wide not just for their beautiful flowers, but for their extraordinary singing voices that could soothe animals into sleep.

One sunny morning, the eldest sister, Lily, woke up with a strange feeling. Today wasn’t just another day; she felt different. Gathering her sisters, they decided to visit the meadow.

As they approached, they discovered a peculiar sight. All the flowers had closed their petals, and several dozing owls perched above. Lily fluttered her wings, sending a soft breeze through the air. The birds fluttered awake, chirping harmoniously. Then something even more magical happened.

From behind a tree came a soft, laughter-like song. The sisters circled cautiously. When they reached where the sound came from, they gasped. Hidden behind the trunk was an elderly lady, gracefully dressed in feathers.

“Ah!” she said softly. “Why do you sing
